turns.content: I'm back in coding after five years and I want to build a search tool using SQLx, but I was thinking full-text search would just be like running a query with string matching on columns. Is that how it works?

That's not quite right; full-text search in PostgreSQL relies on tsvector and tsquery types which tokenize text into terms and handle word proximity, stemming, and ranking using functions like to_tsquery and ts_rank. Think of it like a librarian who doesn't just look for keywords but understands how words relate to each other so you can find documents that are semantically similar even if they don't share exact phrasing. With SQLx you'd connect to the database, use these PostgreSQL-specific types in your queries, and leverage GIN indexes for performance.
